topic: Verification of c7 H1-H3 moderate patches: 'particle horizon'→Hubble horizon, WIN-053 magnetic-cap→co-opted scale-length λ=8,619 km, sector-decomposition escape closed
our_argument_summary: WIN-052 verdict 'Self-Contradicted' (data/wins.json:052:verdict). detail_verdict_text now contains: (a) Hubble horizon (not particle horizon) framing for prong 2's recession-velocity-boundary analogy — H1 c7 patched; (b) WIN-053 cross-citation as 'co-opted scale-length in WIN-053's toroidal-ovoid magnetic field, where the same λ=8,619 km governs both firmament-height H(r) and magnetic-field B(r)' — H2 c7 patched; (c) sector-decomposition escape now closed via the new clause '— and partitioning the parameter's electromagnetic and gravitational roles into separate model sectors would require the dome to publish the two-sector derivation it does not have.' — H3 c7 patched. Three c7 moderate holes are resolved. The structural three-prong argument (positive cosmological-scale claims + Hubble-horizon-borrows-Hubble-flow + same-parameter-double-duty) and the closing forced choice (retract → silence weakens WIN; keep → Self-Contradicted) are preserved. Three c7 minor holes (H4 ρ_A/λ_A density-length elision, H5 third reinterpretation move, H6 WIN-029 9,086/8,537 inconsistency) were not patched per queue reason 'H1/H2/H3 moderate patches applied'; they carry forward.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — WIN-052 detail_verdict_text is structural argument prose, no numerical pipeline.
data_sources_used: Mistele et al. 2024 ApJL 969 L3 DOI 10.3847/2041-8213/ad54b0
data/wins.json:053:detail_evidence (cross-referenced for λ=8,619 km H(r)/B(r) shared-parameter claim)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Sector-decomposition escape (defender pivot: 'firmament_height is BOTH a hard ELF reflector AND a soft galactic-scale horizon if the two regimes are governed by different sectors of the model') was the c7 H3 finding; the H3 patch text now explicitly closes it with the 'two-sector derivation it does not have' clause.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by the H1-H3 patches; the H2 patch's WIN-053 citation ('λ=8,619 km governs both H(r) and B(r)') is verified accurate against WIN-053:detail_evidence ('The same scale length λ = 8,619 km that governs the firmament height H(r) also appears in the magnetic field equation B(r) — a single parameter pressed into service for two structurally unrelated physical phenomena').

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Searched raw-text/monitor.py for WIN-052 / RAR / Mistele / aetheric_medium / ρ_A — no domain, no fetch, no comparison. WIN-052 has no automated monitoring entry; this is consistent with the detail_verdict_text's existing post-hoc test result ('no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all').
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: ρ_A = g_0 ρ_bar / g† is MOND's interpolation relation (Milgrom 1983) with aetheric labels — see detail_evidence: 'If g† is a₀, the dome's formula is not new physics — it is MOND's interpolation relation rewritten with aetheric labels.' Standard explanation: MOND has explained RAR phenomenology since 1983 with a₀ ≈ 1.2×10⁻¹⁰ m/s². The dome's contribution is relabeling, not derivation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: RAR (McGaugh 2016) and weak-lensing extension (Mistele 2024) both predate the dome's adoption of ρ_A=g_0 ρ_bar/g† as a 'unified theory CONSISTENT' entry. Per detail_verdict_text: 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all — the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite — making even the weaker commit-timestamp test inapplicable.' The dome adopted a known empirical relation and labeled it confirmed.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Per detail_evidence: '(a) no equation for aetheric acceleration as a function of baryonic mass derived from its own parameters, (b) no derivation of a₀ from dome geometry, (c) no prediction for RAR slope or scatter width, and (d) no mechanism connecting a medium confined within a ~20,015 km disc to dynamical effects in galaxies millions of light-years away.' The dome publishes ρ_A=g_0 ρ_bar/g† without symbol definitions, units, or derivation from disc_radius/firmament_height.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c7 H4 — PRONG 1 ρ_A (DENSITY) vs λ_A (LENGTH) ELISION. The setup sentence still references 'λ_A = 4,283 Mpc reframed as merely a wave-equation parameter' as the cosmological-scale anchor; prong 1 then introduces 'ρ_A = g_0 ρ_bar / g†' (a density relation) and concludes 'Cosmological-scale parameter plus cosmological-scale empirical confirmation is not agnosticism.' But the parameter inside prong 1 is ρ_A (density, kg/m³), not λ_A (length, Mpc). The 'cosmological-scale parameter' qualifier inside prong 1 leans on the setup's λ_A reference rather than on the in-prong ρ_A claim. A careful defender could pivot: 'ρ_A is a density relation that the dome could apply only within the disc — it doesn't intrinsically carry a galactic length-scale; you're piggybacking on the setup's λ_A reference.' Per queue reason ('H1/H2/H3 moderate patches applied'), this minor was not patched in this round — expected.
holes_found.recommendation: Carry forward from c7. Optional single-clause edit per c7 H4 rec: append after 'as its strongest cosmological confirmation': '— and the RAR data the dome cites span galaxy scales of 1–100 kpc, locking the ρ_A claim to those scales by its empirical anchor'. Defer to operator decision on whether to bundle with the next moderate patch round.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c7 H5 — CLOSING DICHOTOMY HAS A THIRD REINTERPRETATION MOVE. The closing forced-choice 'The defender must choose: retract... or keep them and concede positive cosmological-scale claims without a derivation. Either move concedes the Self-Contradicted verdict.' is rhetorically strong but has a third escape: a defender can REINTERPRET the unified-theory CONSISTENT entry as 'structurally compatible with the dome framework when read as a within-disc relation' and reframe 'strongest cosmological confirmation' as 'the dome's relation captures the same phenomenology the RAR exhibits, whatever the underlying interpretation.' This is a Pyrrhic move (it costs the original confident framing) but it sidesteps the dichotomy. Per queue reason ('H1/H2/H3 moderate patches applied'), this minor was not patched in this round — expected.
holes_found.recommendation: Carry forward from c7. Optional addition per c7 H5 rec: after 'or keep them and concede positive cosmological-scale claims without a derivation' add ' — or reinterpret CONSISTENT as compatible-when-restricted-to-within-disc, which silently retracts the WIN's cosmological-scale claim while pretending not to'. Defer to operator decision.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c7 H6 — WIN-029 9,086/8,537 SELF-INCONSISTENCY NOT FLAGGED INLINE. The patched detail_verdict_text asserts 'firmament_height = 9,086 km' confidently in prong 3, while our own WIN-029:detail_evidence flags an internal dome inconsistency: 'H(r=0) = 8,537 km from its own exponential formula, vs. firmament_height = 9,086 km from the model's stated core parameters — a 6.4% discrepancy never acknowledged'. A pedantic defender can reply: 'You assert 9,086 km confidently here while in WIN-029 you flag that the dome itself publishes 9,086 vs 8,537. Pick one.' Minor self-consistency wrinkle; not patch-introduced and not patch-resolved.
holes_found.recommendation: Carry forward from c7. Optional one-word qualifier or leave as-is per c7 H6 rec. The WIN-052 prong critiques the dome's stated core parameter (9,086 km) and the WIN-029 internal inconsistency (9,086 vs 8,537) is a separate concern — keeping them in separate WINs is defensible. Defer.

holes_found.description: NEW POST-PATCH: H3's sector-decomposition close clause is grammatically loose. The appended text reads '— and partitioning the parameter's electromagnetic and gravitational roles into separate model sectors would require the dome to publish the two-sector derivation it does not have.' The em-dash continuation attaches the sector-decomposition close to the immediately prior phrase 'where the model needs deniability' rather than to the prong's main claim. A reader parsing the sentence may struggle to identify which clause the 'partitioning' move is responding to. The H3 patch correctly closes the escape but the placement could be clearer as a standalone sentence.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional sentence-split. Replace the em-dash with a period: '...where the model needs deniability. Partitioning the parameter's electromagnetic and gravitational roles into separate model sectors would require the dome to publish the two-sector derivation it does not have.' Eight-character delta; improves the prong's standalone readability. Not blocking — the H3 close is structurally correct as written.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c7 H4 (ρ_A vs λ_A elision) — no matching open-issue found in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json grep; decider should create one if H4 is to be tracked rather than absorbed in a future minor-edit round.
c7 H5 (third reinterpretation move) — no matching open-issue found; decider should create one if to be tracked.
c7 H6 (WIN-029 9,086/8,537 inconsistency) — no matching open-issue found; decider's call whether to track or leave as cross-WIN polish.

stronger_arguments.description: The H2 patch's WIN-053 citation is strictly accurate (λ=8,619 km cross-walk confirmed against WIN-053:detail_evidence). A stronger framing could go further by quoting WIN-053's diagnostic verbatim: 'a single parameter pressed into service for two structurally unrelated physical phenomena.' Citing the structural diagnosis (parameter-double-duty as a methodological tell) rather than just the shared-λ value would make prong 3 self-supporting without requiring readers to click into WIN-053. Optional polish, not required for the verdict.
sources: data/wins.json:053:detail_evidence (final paragraph): 'The same scale length λ = 8,619 km that governs the firmament height H(r) also appears in the magnetic field equation B(r) — a single parameter pressed into service for two structurally unrelated physical phenomena.'

kernel_of_truth.description: All three c7 H1-H3 moderate patches landed cleanly and at the right textual locations. H1's particle/Hubble horizon distinction was a textbook factual error in our prose; the fix is one-token and correct. H2's WIN-053 citation now matches what WIN-053 actually argues (shared λ=8,619 km, not 'hard magnetic-cap boundary'). H3's sector-decomposition close lands the EXP-407 anticipated-objection response in the live prose where defenders can see it. The patches collectively trade rhetorical sweep for citation accuracy — the right trade for a self-contradiction verdict that must survive close reading.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: WIN-052's structural argument is unchanged by the patches: the dome publishes a positive cosmological-scale claim (ρ_A=g₀ρ_bar/g† CONSISTENT entry citing RAR as strongest cosmological confirmation per data/wins.json:052:detail_evidence) inside a model whose own physical geometry (20,015 km disc, 9,086 km firmament) cannot accommodate the 1–100 kpc galaxy scales the RAR describes. The Hubble-horizon analogy still borrows the very Hubble flow the dome rejects; the same firmament_height parameter still cannot be soft (galaxy-scale horizon) and hard (Schumann reflector) simultaneously; the sector-decomposition rescue still requires a derivation the dome has not published. The patches fix the prose's accuracy without altering its conclusion. The verdict 'Self-Contradicted' is preserved.

deeper_analysis: Verification of c7 H1/H2/H3 moderate patches against clone HEAD d905eac1. H1: 'particle horizon' → 'Hubble horizon' confirmed via grep — old phrase absent, new phrase present in detail_verdict_text. H2: 'hard magnetic-cap boundary in WIN-053' → 'co-opted scale-length in WIN-053's toroidal-ovoid magnetic field, where the same λ=8,619 km governs both firmament-height H(r) and magnetic-field B(r)' confirmed; cross-checked against WIN-053:detail_evidence which explicitly contains 'The same scale length λ = 8,619 km that governs the firmament height H(r) also appears in the magnetic field equation B(r)' — citation now matches source WIN. H3: sector-decomposition close clause ' — and partitioning the parameter's electromagnetic and gravitational roles into separate model sectors would require the dome to publish the two-sector derivation it does not have.' confirmed appended after 'where the model needs deniability'. Three c7 moderate holes resolved. The c7 H4/H5/H6 (all minor) carry forward as expected per queue reason. One new minor finding: H3's close clause is grammatically loose as an em-dash extension; sentence-split would improve standalone parseability. Verdict 'Self-Contradicted' holds. recommended_action 'no_change' because the patches landed correctly and the verdict is preserved; the c7 minor carry-forwards and the new H3 polish suggestion are operator-discretion future work, not blockers.

citations_verified: H1 'Hubble horizon' replacement — verified present, 'particle horizon' verified absent in detail_verdict_text.
H2 'co-opted scale-length in WIN-053' / 'λ=8,619 km governs both firmament-height H(r) and magnetic-field B(r)' — verified present; cross-checked against WIN-053:detail_evidence final paragraph.

H3 'partitioning the parameter's electromagnetic and gravitational roles into separate model sectors would require the dome to publish the two-sector derivation it does not have' — verified present, appended after 'where the model needs deniability'.

Mistele et al. 2024 DOI 10.3847/2041-8213/ad54b0 — pre-existing, not affected.
